proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and those not receiving it (any direction).
Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_7plus3 and those not receiving it.
Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_midostaurin and those not receiving it.
Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib and those not receiving it.
Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib and those not receiving it.
Mean objective_response rate differs between patients receiving treatment_enasidenib and those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: response 24.0% on vs 18.6% off; chi-square p=6.62e-51, rate-difference=+0.0535.
treatment_7plus3: response 18.9% on vs 18.6% off; p=0.465; rate diff=+0.0026.
treatment_midostaurin: response 18.6% on vs 18.8% off; p=0.673; rate diff=-0.0021.
treatment_gilteritinib: rate diff=-0.0073, p=0.139.
treatment_ivosidenib: rate diff=+0.0007, p=0.928.
treatment_enasidenib: rate diff=-0.0018, p=0.791.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have higher objective_response than those with npm1_mutation=0.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have lower objective_response than those with tp53_mutation=0.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have lower objective_response than those with complex_karyotype=0.
Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a different objective_response from those with secondary_aml=0.
Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response from those with flt3_itd=0.
Patients labelled unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response from those with unfit_for_intensive=0.
Patients with idh1_mutation=1 or idh2_mutation=1 differ in objective_response from wild-type for those genes.

analyses.result_summary: npm1_mutation: response 23.5% (mut) vs 16.7% (wt); rate diff=+0.0674, chi2 p=5.79e-70.
tp53_mutation: response 16.6% (mut) vs 19.0% (wt); rate diff=-0.0242, p=3.51e-05.
complex_karyotype: response 16.8% vs 19.1%; rate diff=-0.0229, p=3.31e-06.
secondary_aml: response 18.8% vs 18.7%; rate diff=+0.0008, p=0.851.
flt3_itd: rate diff=+0.0021, p=0.636.
unfit_for_intensive: response 22.1% (unfit) vs 16.5% (fit); rate diff=+0.0559, p=1.88e-55.
idh1_mutation: rate diff=+0.0037, p=0.596; idh2_mutation: rate diff=+0.0044, p=0.474.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic ecog_ps: beta=-0.3529, p=1.59e-90.
Univariate logistic albumin_g_dl: beta=+0.1175, p=2.86e-07.
Univariate logistic wbc_k_per_ul: beta=-0.0018, p=1.85e-04.
Univariate logistic blast_pct_marrow: beta=-0.0049, p=2.41e-16.
Univariate logistic weight_loss_pct_6mo: beta=-0.0357, p=1.14e-30.
Univariate logistic crp_mg_l: beta=-0.0064, p=6.05e-06.
Univariate logistic age_years: beta=-0.00016, p=0.882.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
Among idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
Among idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
Among flt3_tkd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: midostaurin within flt3_itd=1: rate 17.6% on vs 19.1% off (n_on=1468, n_off=8532); rate diff=-0.0150, p=0.182.
gilteritinib within flt3_itd=1: rate 19.1% on vs 18.9% off; rate diff=+0.0023, p=0.830.
ivosidenib within idh1_mutation=1: rate 15.4% on vs 19.4% off; rate diff=-0.0399, p=0.106.
enasidenib within idh2_mutation=1: rate 19.5% on vs 19.1% off; rate diff=+0.0037, p=0.888.
gilteritinib within flt3_tkd=1: rate diff=-0.0349, p=0.098.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is larger in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients than in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (positive interaction).
The treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response is larger in unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) patients than in unfit=1 patients.
Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response relative to those not receiving it.
Among unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine does NOT increase objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction term venetoclax_aza × unfit (unadjusted): beta=+0.8829, p=1.14e-78.
Logistic interaction term 7+3 × unfit (unadjusted): beta=-0.0273, p=0.556.
venaza within unfit=1: rate 30.8% on vs 16.3% off; rate diff=+0.1451, p=8.87e-128.
venaza within unfit=0 (fit): rate 16.0% on vs 16.8% off; rate diff=-0.0077, p=0.081.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Targeted therapy/mutation match interactions are weak: treatment_midostaurin × flt3_itd, treatment_gilteritinib × flt3_itd, treatment_ivosidenib × idh1_mutation, treatment_enasidenib × idh2_mutation are not significant predictors of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: midostaurin×flt3_itd beta=-0.1063, p=0.195; gilteritinib×flt3_itd beta=+0.0792, p=0.323; ivosidenib×idh1 beta=-0.3042, p=0.077; enasidenib×idh2 beta=+0.0394, p=0.788. All p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the multivariable logistic model adjusted for demographics, mutations, labs and treatments, npm1_mutation is independently associated with higher objective_response.
In the same model, ecog_ps is independently associated with lower objective_response.

In the same model, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is independently associated with higher objective_response (main effect), while the four targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and 7+3 are not.
In the same model, complex_karyotype and tp53_mutation are independently associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic, npm1_mutation: beta=+0.4355 (OR=1.55), p=1.66e-71.
Multivariable logistic, ecog_ps: beta=-0.3615, p=9.08e-93.

Multivariable logistic main effects of treatments: venetoclax_aza beta=+0.3617 (p=8.23e-54); 7+3 beta=+0.0141 (p=0.546); midostaurin -0.0089 (p=0.786); gilteritinib -0.0497 (p=0.131); ivosidenib +0.0194 (p=0.654); enasidenib -0.0110 (p=0.798).
Multivariable logistic: complex_karyotype beta=-0.1688, p=6.14e-07; tp53_mutation beta=-0.1881, p=3.55e-06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When key targeted therapy × biomarker interactions are added to the multivariable model, only treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × unfit_for_intensive is a strong significant interaction (positive); treatment_7plus3 × complex_karyotype is significantly negative; midostaurin/gilteritinib × flt3_itd, ivosidenib × idh1, enasidenib × idh2 are not significant.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interactions: venaza×unfit beta=+0.9046, p=1.72e-80; 7+3×complex_karyotype beta=-0.2373, p=0.0005; midostaurin×flt3_itd p=0.242; gilteritinib×flt3_itd p=0.448; ivosidenib×idh1 p=0.067; enasidenib×idh2 p=0.845.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 6-treatment × 10-binary-modifier interaction screen (each model adjusted for age, sex, ECOG, secondary_aml, unfit, complex_karyotype, tp53, albumin) will surface treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation as the strongest positive heterogeneity term.
Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × tp53_mutation is a significant negative interaction (TP53 attenuates ven/aza benefit).
Treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × complex_karyotype is a significant negative interaction.
treatment_7plus3 × complex_karyotype is a significant negative interaction (complex karyotype attenuates 7+3 effect / makes it harmful).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction venetoclax_aza × npm1: beta=+1.0712, p=2.60e-103 (top hit of the 60-test screen).
Adjusted interaction venetoclax_aza × tp53: beta=-0.4310, p=1.37e-07.
Adjusted interaction venetoclax_aza × complex_karyotype: beta=-0.3157, p=3.45e-06.
Adjusted interaction 7+3 × complex_karyotype: beta=-0.2242, p=0.0009.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effects on objective_response are not strongly modified by continuous covariates age_years, ecog_ps (treated continuously), albumin_g_dl, wbc_k_per_ul, or ldh_u_l (no treatment-by-continuous covariate interaction has p<0.05).
Stratified response in npm1_mutation × flt3_itd cells: response is highest in npm1=1 patients regardless of flt3_itd status (NPM1 prognostic, not modified by FLT3-ITD in this cohort).

analyses.result_summary: 30 treatment×continuous covariate interactions (z-scored covariates): smallest p-value = 0.074; no interaction reaches p<0.05.

npm1=1, flt3_itd=0: response 23.4% (n=12035); npm1=1, flt3_itd=1: 23.5% (n=2979); npm1=0, flt3_itd=0: 16.7% (n=27965); npm1=0, flt3_itd=1: 17.0% (n=7021). FLT3-ITD adds essentially nothing within either NPM1 stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × npm1_mutation is a very large positive interaction (i.e., the within-unfit ven/aza benefit is concentrated in npm1_mutation=1 patients).

Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately 50 percentage points relative to no ven/aza.
Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=0 patients (regardless of tp53_mutation status), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response.
Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response (TP53 abolishes ven/aza benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Within unfit_for_intensive=1, logistic objective_response ~ venaza*npm1: interaction beta=+2.1736, p=3.33e-173.
Cell unfit=1, npm1=1, tp53=0: response on venaza=69.1% (n_on=2125) vs off venaza=15.7% (n_off=3224); Δ=+0.5339, Fisher p=0.00e+00.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by ~63 percentage points (the cleanest 'super-responder' subgroup).

Among unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect (complex karyotype suppresses the benefit even with otherwise-favorable NPM1+/TP53wt).

analyses.result_summary: Cell unfit=1, npm1=1, tp53=0, ck=0: response on=78.6% (n=1790) vs off=15.8% (n=2741); Δ=+0.6277, Fisher p=0.00e+00.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive=0 (fit) AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no benefit (the unfit predicate is required for the ven/aza super-effect).

analyses.result_summary: Cell unfit=0, npm1=1, tp53=0: response on=15.7% (n=3270) vs off=16.1% (n=4889); Δ=-0.0046, p=0.60. Confirms unfit predicate is necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single joint logistic model that includes treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interactions with all four candidate modifiers (unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype) plus baseline covariates, all four interaction terms remain significant with the expected signs (positive for unfit and npm1, negative for tp53 and complex_karyotype).

analyses.result_summary: Joint logistic interactions: venaza×unfit beta=+0.947 (p=1.78e-86); venaza×npm1 beta=+1.113 (p=3.58e-109); venaza×tp53 beta=-0.487 (p=5.04e-09); venaza×complex_karyotype beta=-0.348 (p=5.25e-07). All four significant in expected directions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response across all three ecog_ps strata (0, 1, 2), with similar magnitude (~+19–22 pp).

analyses.result_summary: venaza in npm1=1+tp53=0: ECOG=0 Δ=+0.2152 (p=8.54e-58); ECOG=1 Δ=+0.2084 (p=1.31e-85); ECOG=2 Δ=+0.1872 (p=9.80e-27). Effect persists across ECOG strata, but combined with unfit predicate (which is largely captured by ECOG≥1 in routine practice) the signal is strongest where unfit=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has a small positive effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=0 patients but a significantly negative effect in complex_karyotype=1 patients.
Within complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53_mutation=0 patients, treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response by ~0.8 percentage points.

analyses.result_summary: 7+3 in complex_karyotype=0: Δ=+0.0072 (p=0.060); 7+3 in complex_karyotype=1: Δ=-0.0232 (p=0.008); interaction beta in joint model = -0.2321, p=0.0006.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among idh1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_ivosidenib does NOT increase objective_response and shows a marginal negative trend.
Among idh2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_enasidenib does NOT increase objective_response.
Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_midostaurin does NOT increase objective_response (no benefit detected).
Among flt3_itd=1 patients, treatment_gilteritinib does NOT increase objective_response (no benefit detected).

analyses.result_summary: ivosidenib in idh1+: Δ=-0.0399, p=0.094 (n_on=298, n_off=3326).
enasidenib in idh2+: Δ=+0.0037, p=0.865.
midostaurin in flt3_itd+: Δ=-0.0150, p=0.176.
gilteritinib in flt3_itd+: Δ=+0.0023, p=0.835.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 10 binary biomarker modifiers, treatment_7plus3 shows the strongest heterogeneity with complex_karyotype (negative); other 7+3 × biomarker interactions (npm1, tp53, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, idh1, idh2, sex_female, unfit_for_intensive) are not significant after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted 7+3 × biomarker screen (10 tests): only 7+3 × complex_karyotype reaches p<0.05 (beta=-0.224, p=0.0009); 7+3 × npm1 p=0.13, 7+3 × tp53 p=0.50, 7+3 × secondary_aml p=0.17, 7+3 × idh1 p=0.14, 7+3 × idh2 p=0.60, 7+3 × flt3_itd p=0.48, 7+3 × flt3_tkd p=1.00, 7+3 × sex_female p=0.52, 7+3 × unfit p=0.71.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the full adjusted 6-treatment × 10-modifier screen (60 tests), no treatment_midostaurin × any modifier interaction has p<0.05.
Across the same screen, no treatment_gilteritinib × any modifier interaction has p<0.05 (smallest p≈0.036 for ×unfit_for_intensive).

Across the same screen, treatment_ivosidenib × idh1_mutation has a marginal negative interaction (beta≈−0.35, p≈0.045) and treatment_ivosidenib × npm1_mutation has a marginal negative interaction (beta≈−0.20, p≈0.028); no other ivosidenib interaction is significant.

treatment_enasidenib × sex_female has a positive interaction (beta=+0.31, p=0.0003) — i.e. enasidenib appears to add response in female patients more than male patients, though this is not a primary biological pathway.

analyses.result_summary: Smallest midostaurin × modifier p across 10 tests = 0.125 (×secondary_aml). All NS.
gilteritinib × unfit_for_intensive: beta=+0.137, p=0.0356. Other gilteritinib interactions p>0.10.
ivosidenib×idh1: beta=-0.347, p=0.0451; ivosidenib×npm1: beta=-0.202, p=0.0280.
enasidenib × sex_female: beta=+0.314, p=0.0003 (60-test screen — moderate concern for multiplicity, but signal is largest non-ven/aza/non-7+3 interaction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When ven/aza interactions with unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, and complex_karyotype are jointly fit alongside baseline covariates (and similarly for 7+3 with complex_karyotype, tp53, unfit), only the four ven/aza interactions and the 7+3 × complex_karyotype interaction remain significant.

analyses.result_summary: Joint ven/aza model: all four interactions significant (above). Joint 7+3 model: 7+3×complex_karyotype beta=-0.2321 (p=0.0006); 7+3×tp53 p=0.523; 7+3×unfit p=0.720.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: among patients satisfying unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +0.63 (78.6% on vs 15.8% off). The unfavorable variables tp53_mutation and complex_karyotype each suppress the effect to non-significance even within unfit, NPM1+ patients; the unfit predicate is required (no benefit in fit NPM1+ patients); and the npm1_mutation predicate is required (no benefit in unfit NPM1- patients).

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup test (unfit=1 ∧ npm1=1 ∧ tp53=0 ∧ ck=0): venaza on=78.6% (n=1790) vs off=15.8% (n=2741); Δ=+0.6277, Fisher p=0.00e+00. Sentinel cell-by-cell pattern: in any of the 15 other (unfit×npm1×tp53×ck) cells, |Δ|≤0.04 and not significant; signal is uniquely concentrated in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS for treatment_7plus3: among patients with complex_karyotype=0 AND tp53_mutation=0, treatment_7plus3 modestly increases objective_response by ~0.8 percentage points (19.8% on vs 19.0% off). The complex_karyotype=0 predicate is required because in complex_karyotype=1 patients, 7+3 has a significantly negative effect (15.6% on vs 18.0% off, Δ=−0.023, p≈0.008).

FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT FINDING for treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib: NO subgroup of patients with usable size (n>50 in both arms) shows an objective_response benefit at p<0.05 in the expected direction. Even within the canonical target subgroups (FLT3-ITD for midostaurin/gilteritinib, IDH1 for ivosidenib, IDH2 for enasidenib), the rate differences are essentially zero or marginally negative.

analyses.result_summary: Best targeted-therapy subgroup tests: midostaurin in flt3_itd=1 Δ=-0.0150 (p=0.176); gilteritinib in flt3_itd=1 Δ=+0.0023 (p=0.835); ivosidenib in idh1=1 Δ=-0.0399 (p=0.094); enasidenib in idh2=1 Δ=+0.0037 (p=0.865). None significant in expected direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After the analyses above, the prognostic story (independent of treatment) is: higher npm1_mutation, higher albumin_g_dl reduce risk of non-response; higher ecog_ps, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, blast_pct_marrow, wbc_k_per_ul, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l increase risk of non-response; age_years, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1_mutation, idh2_mutation are not independent prognostic factors after adjustment.

The predictive story (treatment effect heterogeneity) reduces to two findings: (a) treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is highly effective only in unfit_for_intensive=1 ∧ npm1_mutation=1 ∧ tp53_mutation=0 ∧ complex_karyotype=0; (b) treatment_7plus3 is mildly effective in complex_karyotype=0 patients and harmful in complex_karyotype=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs (per unit / per category): npm1 OR=1.55, albumin OR/g≈1.13, ecog OR/unit≈0.70, complex_karyotype OR≈0.84, tp53 OR≈0.83; age, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2 all p>0.4.

Quantitative summary: ven/aza super-responder Δ=+0.63 (78.6% vs 15.8%); ven/aza in any of the 15 other unfit×npm1×tp53×ck cells |Δ|≤0.04 NS. 7+3 in complex_karyotype=0 Δ=+0.007 (NS, marginal +0.008 in tp53-wt subgroup); 7+3 in complex_karyotype=1 Δ=−0.023 (p=0.008). All four targeted therapies show no subgroup-specific benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: OVERALL CONCLUSION: Across the full ds001_aml cohort, the dominant treatment-related signal is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in the (unfit ∧ NPM1+ ∧ TP53-wt ∧ non-complex-karyotype) subgroup; only one other treatment shows any heterogeneity (treatment_7plus3 hurt by complex karyotype); the four targeted therapies show no detectable benefit in any subgroup. The dataset provides clear evidence of biomarker-defined treatment-effect heterogeneity for venetoclax/azacitidine and clear evidence of biomarker-defined treatment-effect heterogeneity (negative direction) for 7+3 in complex karyotype; the lack of targeted-therapy benefit is itself a notable finding.

analyses.result_summary: All hypotheses above triangulate this conclusion. Quantitative anchors: super-responder cell n=4531, Δ=+0.628 in response rate (Fisher p≈0); 7+3 × complex_karyotype interaction beta=−0.232 (p=0.0006) in joint adjusted logistic; targeted therapies × target mutation interactions all p>0.05 in adjusted models with the strongest signal being a marginal NEGATIVE ivosidenib×idh1 (beta=−0.35, p=0.045).
